sentence_reviews.text: Sim-to-real methods train or validate in simulation and transfer behavior to physical systems, often using real-to-sim calibration or residual adaptation to close the reality gap.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Zhao et al. 2020 arXiv abstract: simulation environments train agents, gap between simulated and real worlds degrades transferred policies, methods close sim-to-real gap; Tobin et al. 2017 abstract: simulation-to-real transfer with domain randomization.
sentence_reviews.notes: Training in simulation, transfer, and reality gap are directly supported. Real-to-sim calibration/residual adaptation are not directly inspected in the cited abstracts.

sentence_reviews.text: They reduce the cost and danger of physical interaction while preserving a route to deployment.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Zhao et al. 2020 abstract: real-world data collection has sample inefficiency, cost, and safety concerns; simulation provides potentially infinite data and alleviates safety concerns.
sentence_reviews.notes: Directly supported by the inspected sim-to-real survey abstract.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Zhao et al. 2020 title/abstract: robotics and policy transfer to real robots; Tobin et al. 2017 abstract: object localization stepping stone to robotic manipulation and grasping in clutter.
Zhao et al. 2020 abstract: methods applicable to different domains and application scenarios; no autonomous-driving-specific passage inspected.
sentence_reviews.notes: Autonomous driving is plausible for sim-to-real, but not directly supported by inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Zhao et al. 2020 abstract: closing sim-to-real gap and policy transfer; no digital twin calibration passage inspected.
sentence_reviews.notes: Calibration/digital transfer is plausible, but the specific digital-twin example is not directly evidenced.

sentence_reviews.text: Unmodeled dynamics, sensing artifacts, contacts, and rare events can cause severe transfer failure.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Zhao et al. 2020 abstract: simulated-real gap degrades policy performance; Tobin et al. 2017 abstract: reality gap separates simulated robotics from hardware.
sentence_reviews.notes: Reality-gap failure is supported, but the detailed list of causes is not directly inspected.

sentence_reviews.text: Sim-to-real, real-to-sim & digital transfer follows the learning setup expressed by Transfer, multitask & domain adaptation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Zhao et al. 2020 abstract: policy transfer, domain randomization, domain adaptation; Tobin et al. 2017 title/abstract: transferring DNNs from simulation to real world.

sentence_reviews.text: Sim-to-real, real-to-sim & digital transfer follows the learning setup expressed by Model-based reinforcement learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Zhao et al. 2020 title/abstract: sim-to-real transfer in deep reinforcement learning for robotics; no model-based RL passage inspected.
sentence_reviews.notes: Deep RL is supported, but model-based RL specifically is not directly supported by inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Sim-to-real, real-to-sim & digital transfer uses Multimodal, simulated & streaming data as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Zhao et al. 2020 abstract: simulation environments for training agents; Tobin et al. 2017 abstract: simulated images transferred to real images.
sentence_reviews.notes: Simulated data is directly supported; multimodal/streaming is broader than inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Sim-to-real, real-to-sim & digital transfer uses Dynamical systems & control theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Zhao et al. 2020 abstract: robotics, agents, policy transfer to real robots; Tobin et al. 2017 abstract: robotic control.
sentence_reviews.notes: Robotic control is supported, but dynamical systems/control theory as a dependency is not directly established by inspected evidence.

edge_reviews.notes: Transfer from simulation to real world and domain adaptation/randomization directly support this core trained_under edge.
The inspected source supports deep RL, not model-based RL specifically; common strength may be plausible but under-supported.
Simulated data is core/common, but multimodal and streaming aspects are not directly supported.
Robotic control supports the direction and uses relation, but the source does not directly establish control theory as a common dependency.
